title: Emergency & Critical Care
subtitle: Life-Saving Expertise
description: Specialized training for emergency situations and critical care, preparing nurses for high-pressure medical scenarios.

detail.mission_text: To produce highly skilled emergency and critical care nurses capable of providing rapid, evidence-based interventions in life-threatening situations while maintaining compassion and professional excellence.
detail.intro: The Department of Emergency and Critical Care Nursing at St. Paul's Hospital Millennium Medical College is dedicated to training nurses who can excel in high-pressure medical environments. Our program combines advanced theoretical knowledge with extensive hands-on clinical experience to prepare graduates for the challenges of emergency medicine and intensive care units.

areas.title: Emergency Nursing
areas.description: Rapid assessment and intervention for acute medical conditions, trauma care, and emergency department operations.

areas.title: Critical Care Nursing
areas.description: Advanced care for critically ill patients in ICU/CCU settings, including ventilator management and hemodynamic monitoring.

areas.title: Trauma Nursing
areas.description: Specialized care for trauma patients, including mass casualty incidents and disaster response protocols.

areas.title: Acute Care
areas.description: Management of acute exacerbations of chronic conditions and rapid deterioration scenarios in various clinical settings.

training.title: Simulation Labs
training.description: State-of-the-art simulation centers with high-fidelity manikins for emergency scenarios, code blue drills, and critical care skill development.

training.title: Clinical Rotations
training.description: Extensive clinical rotations through emergency departments, intensive care units, and trauma centers for real-world experience.

training.title: Advanced Skills Labs
training.description: Specialized skills training for advanced procedures, equipment operation, and emergency intervention techniques.
